name: DX (GetDX) Developer Experience API
what_it_does: Developer experience platform with REST API — enabling engineering leaders to measure developer sentiment and productivity via surveys (Developer Experience Index), track DORA metrics, analyze CI/CD performance, and correlate tooling/process changes with developer satisfaction, with GitHub/GitLab integration and Slack survey delivery.
use_cases: Measuring developer experience via DX API for sentiment and productivity correlation analysis
Tracking DORA metrics via DX API for engineering delivery performance benchmarking
Running DXI (Developer Experience Index) surveys via DX API for team health measurement
Analyzing CI/CD pipeline performance via DX API for bottleneck identification
Exporting DX metrics via API for engineering leadership dashboards and OKR tracking
not_for: Individual developer performance reviews (DX focuses on system/team-level improvement)
Real-time code review or task management
Teams without GitHub or GitLab integration
best_when: An engineering leader wants to combine developer sentiment surveys with DORA metrics to understand what's actually slowing teams down — correlating subjective developer experience with objective delivery data.
avoid_when: You need individual surveillance metrics, work without GitHub/GitLab, or need a fully self-serve free tier.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from DX account settings. REST API at api.getdx.com. Webhooks for survey response events. GitHub/GitLab OAuth integration for DORA data. Slack integration for survey delivery. No OAuth2 for third-party access.

pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco). Founded 2021 by Abi Noda and Laura Tacho. $16M+ funding (Andreessen Horowitz). Rebranded from GetDX to DX in 2023. Developer Experience Index (DXI): proprietary survey framework based on SPACE framework (Satisfaction, Performance, Activity, Collaboration, Efficiency). GitHub/GitLab DORA metrics: deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, MTTR. CI/CD analytics: build times, flaky tests, pipeline bottlenecks. Slack integration: survey delivery via Slack. Used by: Stripe, Block, Twilio, Shopify. Academic partnership with Dr. Nicole Forsgren (DORA creator).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. DORA + DXI surveys. GitHub/GitLab required. Webhooks for survey events. SOC2/GDPR. Andreessen Horowitz backed. Nicole Forsgren academic partnership.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Metric reads are idempotent — historical DORA and DXI data is deterministic. Survey reads idempotent by survey ID. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: Rebranded from GetDX to DX (2023) — documentation may reference both names
DXI (Developer Experience Index) surveys are team-level — no individual developer data
GitHub/GitLab integration must be configured before DORA metrics available via API
Webhooks for survey responses — implement idempotent handlers for survey events
US data residency — verify GDPR compliance for EU teams (GDPR certified but US hosted)
Contact sales for API access — not self-serve for API tier

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token API key. SOC2 and GDPR certified. Developer experience data (survey responses, productivity metrics) is sensitive HR-adjacent data. GitHub/GitLab integration credentials must be secured. Slack integration for survey delivery — manage Slack app permissions. No OAuth2 for fine-grained third-party access.
